payload.answer: Recall returned mem_new because it is the newer memory for Sam from Goldman. mem_old was filtered out because it was superseded, so it is no longer the current memory to present.
citations: Input: "Explain why recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman and filtered mem_old because it is superseded."
payload.decision: Recall should prefer mem_new because it is the latest unsuperseded memory for the same entity/context, while mem_old is older and marked by the recall logic as superseded, so it should not be presented as current.

entity_resolution.reason: The explanation refers to Sam associated with Goldman, but no stable entity identifier is provided in the input.

payload.intent: Explain a recall result for Sam from Goldman, specifically why the newer memory was returned and the older memory was filtered out as superseded.

memory_cards.statement: Recall returned mem_new for Sam from Goldman because it is the newer, current memory in the set.
mem_old was filtered out because it was superseded and should not be treated as the active fact.

details: Identified mem_new as the current returned memory.
Identified mem_old as superseded and therefore filtered.
Preserved ambiguity by not inventing an entity ID.

repair_options: If you want stricter provenance, attach entity IDs for Sam and Goldman.
If you want a fuller audit trail, include the superseding relation that marks mem_old as obsolete.
If mem_old should still be visible for history, mark it as historical instead of current.
